meta.description: Impute missing values in expression or clinical data matrices using k-nearest neighbor imputation (impute.knn or KNNImputer). Inputs: matrix with missing values, k parameter. Outputs: imputed matrix, missingness summary, pre/post distribution QC report.

key_strengths: Clear scope boundaries reduce accidental misuse on unsupported data types and workflows.
The implementation is reproducible, with a fixed seed and session metadata saved as an artifact.
Error handling is specific and actionable, using stable SKILL_* messages across multiple failure modes.
The documented small-strata fallback and stratum-aware behavior matched live execution results.

recommendations.title: Persist run summary metadata
recommendations.problem: Important QC details such as removed-gene count, per-stratum method choice, and unresolved-value count are emitted only to stdout. If users do not capture logs, key provenance is lost after a successful run.
recommendations.root_cause: Operational metadata is written as transient console output instead of a stable artifact.
recommendations.fix: Write a small run_summary.json or run_summary.txt next to the imputed matrix and session info. Include removed features, stratum sizes, method used per stratum, effective k, imputed value count, unresolved count, and seed.

recommendations.title: Surface runtime expectations in SKILL.md
recommendations.problem: The main skill page is operationally clear, but runtime and resource expectations are mostly documented in cli-guide.md. Users who only read SKILL.md may underestimate execution cost on larger matrices.
recommendations.root_cause: Performance guidance is concentrated in a secondary reference instead of the primary operating instructions.
recommendations.fix: Add a short runtime note near Usage or Local Validation in SKILL.md. Point users to timeout_seconds and lower k as first-response mitigations for larger datasets.

recommendations.title: Validate blank feature identifiers
recommendations.problem: Duplicated feature IDs are rejected, but blank or missing feature identifiers are not explicitly validated before matrix construction. That can make malformed inputs harder to diagnose.
recommendations.root_cause: Input validation is strongest for sample columns and group metadata, with less explicit checking on the feature-ID column.
recommendations.fix: Reject empty or missing feature IDs with a dedicated SKILL_INVALID_DATA message before building the matrix, and document the requirement in the Input Format section.
